commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For they have gone up to Assyria, a wild donkey alone by itself: Ephraim has hired lovers. It is very jealous of liberty, (Job xi. ) and of its females, so that it prevents the young males from becoming its rivals. (Pliny, viii. ; Solin xxx. ) If this were true, the species would soon perish. (Haydock) The Israelites disdained subjection to strangers. They even rejected God, their king; for which reason he abandons them to servitude, in a foreign land. They had run furiously after idols, and had given presents to such lovers.
